summary: High‑energy fall ’71 show with Keith’s debut and a flood of first‑time songs, captured on an overall solid soundboard tape.
review: The October 19 1971 concert is celebrated for its historic firsts – Keith Godchaux’s debut, six brand‑new originals (Tennessee Jed, Jack Straw, Mexicali Blues, Comes A Time, One More Saturday Night and Ramble On Rose) and standout performances of The Other One suite, Wharf Rat and Cryptical.  Most listeners praise the crisp soundboard source, though a few note occasional distortion, so the recording is solid if not flawless.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (over 30) are glowing about both performance and historic significance; only a handful complain about distortion, giving an overall strong show rating with good technical quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers call the tape crisp, high‑fidelity and pre‑FM (better than thin FM recordings). A few note distortion or dropouts, but these are outliers.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A historic first‑show vibe – Keith’s debut, six original debuts and a tight, high‑tempo set that moves fluidly from new material into classic jams, keeping the energy relentless throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for strong guitar work and vocal moments (falsetto on Comes A Time, energetic jamming on One More Saturday Night), with one minor complaint about a vocal slip in 'Playing In The Band'.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mixed notes – some feel his bass sounds thinner than expected, but no major criticism; overall solid foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Generally positive (vocals and rhythm work noted), though one reviewer mentioned he sounded off‑key during a section of 'Playing In The Band'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s debut is highlighted as a major plus – killer piano, unique organ style, even a striking left/right channel separation; a few reviewers feel the keyboard mix can sound scattered.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann receives high praise for an “in‑the‑pocket” groove and inventive leads before The Other One; his drumming on Cryptical is singled out as especially strong.
